title: Andreas Matthies
text: Home * People * Andreas Matthies Andreas Matthies [1] Andreas Matthies , a German computer scientist and computer chess programmer. He had his first computer chess experience at age 13 with a Sargon II chess cartridge for the Commodore VIC-20 . At age 16, he wrote a Connect Four program for the Commodore 64 , and later had some game theory lessons at the university - he graduated from University of Kiel in 1995 [2] . His interest in writing a chess engine revived much later, when someone pointed him to TCEC and the chess programming scene [3] , yielding to the development of RubiChess .
